title: Jastus Farr
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Farr}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Jastusfarr.jpg]] |name=Jastus Farr |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]][[Purge (comic book)|''Purge'']] |species=[[Unidentified brutish blue species]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Blue]] |feathers= |eyes=White |skin=Blue |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Can one take the dark path to achieve good? Or must the dark side inevitably corrupt the good that one hopes to achieve? So the great Masters have always said. 'Dark path, dark end.' That's the common wisdom. Is it the truth, I wonder?|Jastus Farr|}} '''Jastus Farr''' was an [[Unidentified brutish blue species|alien]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served as a [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Farr survived the initial stages of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|order]] to all [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] to [[Death/Legends|murder]] their [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi Generals]]. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Farr [[Conclave on Kessel|met]] with [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[Koffi Arana]], [[Tsui Choi/Legends|Tsui Choi]], [[Roblio Darté]], [[Ma'kis'shaalas/Legends|Ma'kis'shaalas]], and [[Shadday Potkin/Legends|Shadday Potkin]], as well as [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Bultar Swan/Legends|Bultar Swan]] and [[Sia-Lan Wezz]], in an [[Unidentified Kessel spice mine|abandoned spice mine]] on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. However, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] arrived, and Farr and his fellow Jedi engaged the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]. After Wezz, Ma'kis'shaalas, Potkin, Swan, and Arana were killed, Farr, Choi, and Darté combined their efforts in an attempt to eliminate Vader. However, as they prepared to end the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord's]] life, [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] of the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] entered the [[Mining/Legends|mine]] and opened fire with their [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], killing Farr. Choi and Darté were subsequently killed by Vader and the stormtroopers shortly after Farr's death. ==Biography== ===Order 66=== The [[Unidentified brutish blue species|alien]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] Jastus Farr served the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as a [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|issued]] [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], which commanded the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Republic Army]] to [[Death/Legends|eliminate]] the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]].{{Film|III}} Farr survived the beginning stages of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], as the now-[[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine transformed the Republic into the first [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. ===Ambush on Kessel=== {{Quote|What is it you were looking for when you called us together, Master Shadday?''"
"''I expected that we Jedi would do as we always do. We would gather and examine the idea, think about it, perhaps meditate on it, and talk it through. And nothing would be done, or, if done, then done too ''late'' to matter. Which is why I made my final 'invitation.' |Jastus Farr and Shadday Potkin|}} [[File:JastusFarrSlash.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Jastus Farr uses his lightsaber to injure Darth Vader during the Ambush on Kessel.]] A month after the execution of Order 66,{{InsiderCite|87|Order 66: Destroy All Jedi}} Farr made his way to the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. There, he [[Conclave on Kessel|met]] with Jedi Masters [[Koffi Arana]], [[Tsui Choi/Legends|Tsui Choi]], [[Roblio Darté]], [[Ma'kis'shaalas/Legends|Ma'kis'shaalas]], and [[Shadday Potkin/Legends|Shadday Potkin]], and Jedi Knights [[Bultar Swan/Legends|Bultar Swan]] and [[Sia-Lan Wezz]], in an [[Unidentified Kessel spice mine|abandoned spice mine]]. After the Jedi discussed the rise of the Empire and the betrayal of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], Potkin revealed to the Jedi that she had leaked false intelligence that the Jedi Master [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] would be attending the meeting and added that the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] was coming to the meeting in hopes of finding Kenobi. As Farr and the Jedi prepared for battle, Vader stormed the room and demanded to know where Kenobi was. Farr and the Jedi engaged Vader in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]]. During the fight, Farr injured Vader by slashing the back of [[Darth Vader's armor/Legends|the Sith's armor]] with his [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]]. Vader retreated and killed Potkin, stealing her [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Vibroblade/Legends|blade]]. After Vader used the blade to deactivate Farr's, Darté's, and Arana's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], Choi sliced off Vader's sword arm while Swan slashed his left leg. As Vader feigned surrender, Swan and Arana argued about whether or not to kill the Sith. Arana killed Swan so he could use her lightsaber to execute Vader, but the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] then killed Arana. Vader offered Farr, Choi, and Darté one final chance to tell him where Kenobi was. When the three Jedi refused, Farr combined his [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] with Choi and Darté's, [[Telekinesis/Legends|throwing]] debris against Vader. After critically wounding the Dark Lord, Choi prepared to kill Vader. Before he could do so, however, [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] of the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] entered the mine via the catwalks and opened fire on the three Jedi. Their [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire killed Farr, and Vader and the stormtroopers killed Choi and Darté. ==Personality and traits== [[File:FarrDeath.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Farr is cut down by stormtrooper fire.]] Jastus Farr was a large male who had [[Color/Legends|blue]] fur and skin. He owned and used a double-bladed lightsaber, which contained a green [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]]. During the Clone Wars, Farr defended the Republic as a Jedi General. When Chancellor Palpatine issued Order 66, Farr evaded the [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] and subsequently avoided the forces of the Galactic Empire. During his meeting with the other Jedi on Kessel, Farr reminded them that the Jedi Masters stated that the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] inevitably corrupted the good an individual strove to achieve, and he openly questioned the truth of this belief. After Vader had defeated five of the Jedi, Farr—along with Masters Choi and Darté—refused to tell the Dark Lord where Obi-Wan Kenobi was, even though he knew this would lead to Vader attempting to kill him. ==Powers and abilities== Farr was skilled in the use of a lightsaber. When he and his fellow Jedi battled Vader, Farr injured the Dark Lord with his lightsaber. He was also talented in telekinetic Force powers, combining his efforts with Tsui Choi and Roblio Darté to throw debris at Vader. ==Behind the scenes== Jastus Farr was introduced in the [[2005]] standalone [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] [[Purge (comic book)|''Star Wars: Purge'']], written by [[John Ostrander]] and drawn by [[Douglas Wheatley]]. Farr is similar in appearance to the [[Jedi brute]]s in the [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|video game adaptation]] of the film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' video game]] ''Star Wars: Purge'' dates its events, including Farr's death, at one month after the events of ''Revenge of the Sith'', which takes place midway through 19 BBY, as established in 2005's ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Despite this, Keeper of the [[Holocron continuity database]] [[Leland Chee]] stated in a [[2006]] blog entry that the comic's events take place in [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]].{{Blog|holocron|22/comments|Movie Characters killed in the EU}} Several other dates given in the blog entry are incorrect, and as such, this article treats the 19 BBY date as [[canon]]ical. ==Appearances== *[[Purge (comic book)|''Purge'']] {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Special Edition]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Jastus Farr}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Jastus Farr |de=Jastus Farr |es=Jastus Farr |nl=Jastus Farr |pl=Jastus Farr |pt=Jastus Farr }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Farr, Jastus}} [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species]] [[Category:Jedi Generals]] [[Category:Jedi Purge victims]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Post-Ruusan Jedi Masters]]
